title: Last Substring in Lexicographical Order
description:
Given a string s, return the last substring of s in lexicographical order.
Example 1:
Example 2:
Constraints:
constraints: s contains only lowercase English letters.

examples.example_text:
Input: s = "abab"
Output: "bab"
Explanation: The substrings are ["a", "ab", "aba", "abab", "b", "ba", "bab"]. The lexicographically maximum substring is "bab".

hints: Assume that the answer is a sub-string from index i to j. If you add the character at index j+1 you get a better answer.
The answer is always a suffix of the given string.
Since the limits are high, we need an efficient data structure.
